verses.latin_vulgate: Sapiens mulier ædificat domum suam ;  insipiens exstructam quoque manibus destruet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חַכְמ֣וֹת נָ֭שִׁים בָּנְתָ֣ה בֵיתָ֑הּ וְ֝אִוֶּ֗לֶת בְּיָדֶ֥יהָ תֶהֶרְסֶֽנּוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: A wise woman buildeth her house: but the foolish will pull down with her hands that also which is built.
verses.finnish_biblia: Viisas vaimo rakentaa huoneensa, vaan hullu kukistaa sen teollansa.
verses.king_james: Every wise woman buildeth her house: but the foolish plucketh it down with her hands.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ambulans recto itinere, et timens Deum,  despicitur ab eo qui infami graditur via.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הוֹלֵ֣ךְ בְּ֭יׇשְׁרוֹ יְרֵ֣א יְהֹוָ֑ה וּנְל֖וֹז דְּרָכָ֣יו בּוֹזֵֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: He that walketh in the right way, and feareth God, is despised by him that goeth by an infamous way.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka vaeltaa oikiaa tietä, se pelkää Herraa; mutta se, joka poikkee pois tieltänsä, ylönkatsoo hänen.
verses.king_james: He that walketh in his uprightness feareth the Lord: but he that is perverse in his ways despiseth him.

verses.latin_vulgate: In ore stulti virga superbiæ ;  labia autem sapientium custodiunt eos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּֽפִי־אֱ֭וִיל חֹ֣טֶר גַּאֲוָ֑ה וְשִׂפְתֵ֥י חֲ֝כָמִ֗ים תִּשְׁמוּרֵֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: In the mouth of a fool is the rod of pride: but the lips of the wise preserve them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tyhmän suussa on ylpeyden vitsa; vaan viisasten huulet varjelevat heitä.
verses.king_james: In the mouth of the foolish is a rod of pride: but the lips of the wise shall preserve them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ubi non sunt boves, præsepe vacuum est ;  ubi autem plurimæ segetes, ibi manifesta est fortitudo bovis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּאֵ֣ין אֲ֭לָפִים אֵב֣וּס בָּ֑ר וְרׇב־תְּ֝בוּא֗וֹת בְּכֹ֣חַ שֽׁוֹר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Where there are no oxen, the crib is empty: but where there is much corn, there the strength of the ox is manifest.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jossa ei härkiä ole, siinä seimet puhtaana ovat; vaan jossa juhdat työtä tekevät, siinä tuloa kyllä on.
verses.king_james: Where no oxen are, the crib is clean: but much increase is by the strength of the ox.

verses.latin_vulgate: Testis fidelis non mentitur ;  profert autem mendacium dolosus testis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֵ֣ד אֱ֭מוּנִים לֹ֣א יְכַזֵּ֑ב וְיָפִ֥יחַ כְּ֝זָבִ֗ים עֵ֣ד שָֽׁקֶר׃
verses.douay_rheims: A faithful witness will not lie: but a deceitful witness uttereth a lie.
verses.finnish_biblia: Totinen todistaja välttää valhetta; vaan väärä todistaja rohkiasti valhettelee.
verses.king_james: A faithful witness will not lie: but a false witness will utter lies.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quærit derisor sapientiam, et non invenit ;  doctrina prudentium facilis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּקֶּשׁ־לֵ֣ץ חׇכְמָ֣ה וָאָ֑יִן וְדַ֖עַת לְנָב֣וֹן נָקָֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: A scorner seeketh wisdom, and findeth it not: the learning of the wise is easy.
verses.finnish_biblia: Pilkkaaja etsii viisautta, ja ei löydä; vaan toimelliset viisauden huokiasti saavat.
verses.king_james: A scorner seeketh wisdom, and findeth it not: but knowledge is easy unto him that understandeth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vade contra virum stultum,  et nescit labia prudentiæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֵ֣ךְ מִ֭נֶּגֶד לְאִ֣ישׁ כְּסִ֑יל וּבַל־יָ֝דַ֗עְתָּ שִׂפְתֵי־דָֽעַת׃
verses.douay_rheims: Go against a foolish man, and he knoweth not the lips of prudence.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mene pois tyhmän tyköä; sillä et sinä opi mitään häneltä.
verses.king_james: Go from the presence of a foolish man, when thou perceivest not in him the lips of knowledge.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sapientia callidi est intelligere viam suam,  et imprudentia stultorum errans.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חׇכְמַ֣ת עָ֭רוּם הָבִ֣ין דַּרְכּ֑וֹ וְאִוֶּ֖לֶת כְּסִילִ֣ים מִרְמָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: The wisdom of a discreet man is to understand his way: and the imprudence of fools erreth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Toimellisen viisaus on teistänsä ottaa vaarin; vaan tyhmäin hulluus on sula petos.
verses.king_james: The wisdom of the prudent is to understand his way: but the folly of fools is deceit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stultus illudet peccatum,  et inter justos morabitur gratia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֱ֭וִלִים יָלִ֣יץ אָשָׁ֑ם וּבֵ֖ין יְשָׁרִ֣ים רָצֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: A fool will laugh at sin, but among the just grace shall abide.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tyhmä nauraa syntiä, mutta hurskasten välillä on hyvä suosio.
verses.king_james: Fools make a mock at sin: but among the righteous there is favour.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cor quod novit amaritudinem animæ suæ,  in gaudio ejus non miscebitur extraneus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֵ֗ב י֭וֹדֵעַ מׇרַּ֣ת נַפְשׁ֑וֹ וּ֝בְשִׂמְחָת֗וֹ לֹא־יִתְעָ֥רַב זָֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: The heart that knoweth the bitterness of his own soul, in his joy the stranger shall not intermeddle.
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska sydän on murheellinen, niin ei auta ulkonainen ilo.
verses.king_james: The heart knoweth his own bitterness; and a stranger doth not intermeddle with his joy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Domus impiorum delebitur :  tabernacula vero justorum germinabunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בֵּ֣ית רְ֭שָׁעִים יִשָּׁמֵ֑ד וְאֹ֖הֶל יְשָׁרִ֣ים יַפְרִֽיחַ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The house of the wicked shall be destroyed: but the tabernacles of the just shall flourish.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jumalattomain huoneet kukistetaan, vaan jumalisten majat viheriöitsevät.
verses.king_james: The house of the wicked shall be overthrown: but the tabernacle of the upright shall flourish.

verses.latin_vulgate: Est via quæ videtur homini justa,  novissima autem ejus deducunt ad mortem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יֵ֤שׁ דֶּ֣רֶךְ יָ֭שָׁר לִפְנֵי־אִ֑ישׁ וְ֝אַחֲרִיתָ֗הּ דַּרְכֵי־מָֽוֶת׃
verses.douay_rheims: There is a way which seemeth just to a man: but the ends thereof lead to death.
verses.finnish_biblia: Monella on tie mielestänsä oikia, vaan viimeiseltä johdattaa se kuolemaan.
verses.king_james: There is a way which seemeth right unto a man, but the end thereof are the ways of death.

verses.latin_vulgate: Risus dolore miscebitur,  et extrema gaudii luctus occupat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־בִּשְׂחֹ֥ק יִכְאַב־לֵ֑ב וְאַחֲרִיתָ֖הּ שִׂמְחָ֣ה תוּגָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Laughter shall be mingled with sorrow, and mourning taketh hold of the ends of joy.
verses.finnish_biblia: Naurun jälkeen tulee murhe, ja ilon perästä suru.
verses.king_james: Even in laughter the heart is sorrowful; and the end of that mirth is heaviness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Viis suis replebitur stultus,  et super eum erit vir bonus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִדְּרָכָ֣יו יִ֭שְׂבַּע ס֣וּג לֵ֑ב וּ֝מֵעָלָ֗יו אִ֣ישׁ טֽוֹב׃
verses.douay_rheims: A fool shall be filled with his own ways, and the good man shall be above him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tyhmälle tapahtuu laittamisensa jälkeen, vaan hyvä ihminen asetetaan hänen ylitsensä.
verses.king_james: The backslider in heart shall be filled with his own ways: and a good man shall be satisfied from himself.

verses.latin_vulgate: Innocens credit omni verbo ;  astutus considerat gressus suos.  Filio doloso nihil erit boni ;  servo autem sapienti prosperi erunt actus,  et dirigetur via ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פֶּ֭תִי יַאֲמִ֣ין לְכׇל־דָּבָ֑ר וְ֝עָר֗וּם יָבִ֥ין לַאֲשֻׁרֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The innocent believeth every word: the discreet man considereth his steps. No good shall come to the deceitful son: but the wise servant shall prosper in his dealings, and his way shall be made straight.
verses.finnish_biblia: Taitamatoin uskoo kaikki, mutta ymmärtäväinen ottaa teistänsä vaarin.
verses.king_james: The simple believeth every word: but the prudent man looketh well to his going.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sapiens timet, et declinat a malo ;  stultus transilit, et confidit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חָכָ֣ם יָ֭רֵא וְסָ֣ר מֵרָ֑ע וּ֝כְסִ֗יל מִתְעַבֵּ֥ר וּבוֹטֵֽחַ׃
verses.douay_rheims: A wise man feareth, and declineth from evil: the fool leapeth over, and is confident.
verses.finnish_biblia: Viisas pelkää ja karttaa pahaa, vaan tyhmä päätähavin menee.
verses.king_james: A wise man feareth, and departeth from evil: but the fool rageth, and is confident.

verses.latin_vulgate: Impatiens operabitur stultitiam,  et vir versutus odiosus est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קְֽצַר־אַ֭פַּיִם יַעֲשֶׂ֣ה אִוֶּ֑לֶת וְאִ֥ישׁ מְ֝זִמּ֗וֹת יִשָּׂנֵֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: The impatient man shall work folly: and the crafty man is hateful.
verses.finnish_biblia: Äkillinen ihminen tekee hullun töitä, ja kavala ihminen tulee vihattavaksi.
verses.king_james: He that is soon angry dealeth foolishly: and a man of wicked devices is hated.

verses.latin_vulgate: Possidebunt parvuli stultitiam,  et exspectabunt astuti scientiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נָחֲל֣וּ פְתָאיִ֣ם אִוֶּ֑לֶת וַ֝עֲרוּמִ֗ים יַכְתִּ֥רוּ דָֽעַת׃
verses.douay_rheims: The childish shall possess folly, and the prudent shall look for knowledge.
verses.finnish_biblia: Taitamattomat perivät tyhmyyden; vaan se on toimellisten kruunu, että he toimellisesti tekevät.
verses.king_james: The simple inherit folly: but the prudent are crowned with knowledge.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jacebunt mali ante bonos,  et impii ante portas justorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שַׁח֣וּ רָ֭עִים לִפְנֵ֣י טוֹבִ֑ים וּ֝רְשָׁעִ֗ים עַֽל־שַׁעֲרֵ֥י צַדִּֽיק׃
verses.douay_rheims: The evil shall fall down before the good: and the wicked before the gates of the just.
verses.finnish_biblia: Häijyn täytyy kumartaa hyviä, ja jumalattomat vanhurskasten porteissa.
verses.king_james: The evil bow before the good; and the wicked at the gates of the righteous.

verses.latin_vulgate: Etiam proximo suo pauper odiosus erit :  amici vero divitum multi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־לְ֭רֵעֵהוּ יִשָּׂ֣נֵא רָ֑שׁ וְאֹהֲבֵ֖י עָשִׁ֣יר רַבִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: The poor man shall be hateful even to his own neighbour: but the friends of the rich are many.
verses.finnish_biblia: Köyhää vihaavat hänen lähimmäisensäkin; vaan rikkaalla on monta ystävää.
verses.king_james: The poor is hated even of his own neighbour: but the rich hath many friends.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui despicit proximum suum peccat ;  qui autem miseretur pauperis beatus erit.  Qui credit in Domino misericordiam diligit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בׇּז־לְרֵעֵ֥הוּ חוֹטֵ֑א וּמְחוֹנֵ֖ן (עניים) [עֲנָוִ֣ים] אַשְׁרָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: He that despiseth his neighbour, sinneth: but he that sheweth mercy to the poor, shall be blessed. He that believeth in the Lord, loveth mercy.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka katsoo lähimmäisensä ylön. hän tekee syntiä; vaan autuas on se, joka viheliäistä armahtaa.
verses.king_james: He that despiseth his neighbour sinneth: but he that hath mercy on the poor, happy is he.

verses.latin_vulgate: Errant qui operantur malum ;  misericordia et veritas præparant bona.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲֽלוֹא־יִ֭תְעוּ חֹ֣רְשֵׁי רָ֑ע וְחֶ֥סֶד וֶ֝אֱמֶ֗ת חֹ֣רְשֵׁי טֽוֹב׃
verses.douay_rheims: They err that work evil: but mercy and truth prepare good things.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jotka viekkaudessa vaeltavat, niiltä puuttuu; mutta jotka hyvää ajattelevat, niille tapahtuu hyvyys ja uskollisuus.
verses.king_james: Do they not err that devise evil? but mercy and truth shall be to them that devise good.

verses.latin_vulgate: In omni opere erit abundantia ;  ubi autem verba sunt plurima, ibi frequenter egestas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּכׇל־עֶ֭צֶב יִהְיֶ֣ה מוֹתָ֑ר וּדְבַר־שְׂ֝פָתַ֗יִם אַךְ־לְמַחְסֽוֹר׃
verses.douay_rheims: In much work there shall be abundance: but where there are many words, there is oftentimes want.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jossa työtä tehdään, siinä kyllä on; vaan joka tyhjiin puheisiin tyytyy, siinä on köyhyys.
verses.king_james: In all labour there is profit: but the talk of the lips tendeth only to penury.

verses.latin_vulgate: Corona sapientium divitiæ eorum ;  fatuitas stultorum imprudentia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֲטֶ֣רֶת חֲכָמִ֣ים עׇשְׁרָ֑ם אִוֶּ֖לֶת כְּסִילִ֣ים אִוֶּֽלֶת׃
verses.douay_rheims: The crown of the wise, is their riches: the folly of fools, imprudence.
verses.finnish_biblia: Viisasten rikkaus on heidän kruununsa, mutta tyhmäin hulluus on hulluus.
verses.king_james: The crown of the wise is their riches: but the foolishness of fools is folly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Liberat animas testis fidelis,  et profert mendacia versipellis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַצִּ֣יל נְ֭פָשׁוֹת עֵ֣ד אֱמֶ֑ת וְיָפִ֖חַ כְּזָבִ֣ים מִרְמָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: A faithful witness delivereth souls: and the double dealer uttereth lies.
verses.finnish_biblia: Uskollinen todistaja vapahtaa hengen, vaan väärä todistaja pettää.
verses.king_james: A true witness delivereth souls: but a deceitful witness speaketh lies.

verses.latin_vulgate: In timore Domini fiducia fortitudinis,  et filiis ejus erit spes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּיִרְאַ֣ת יְ֭הֹוָה מִבְטַח־עֹ֑ז וּ֝לְבָנָ֗יו יִהְיֶ֥ה מַחְסֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: In the fear of the Lord is confidence of strength, and there shall be hope for his children.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka Herraa pelkää, hänellä on vahva linna, ja hänen lapsensa varjellaan.
verses.king_james: In the fear of the Lord is strong confidence: and his children shall have a place of refuge.

verses.latin_vulgate: Timor Domini fons vitæ,  ut declinent a ruina mortis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִרְאַ֣ת יְ֭הֹוָה מְק֣וֹר חַיִּ֑ים לָ֝ס֗וּר מִמֹּ֥קְשֵׁי מָֽוֶת׃
verses.douay_rheims: The fear of the Lord is a fountain of life, to decline from the ruin of death.
verses.finnish_biblia: Herran pelko on elämän lähde, että kuoleman nuora välttää taidetaan.
verses.king_james: The fear of the Lord is a fountain of life, to depart from the snares of death.

verses.latin_vulgate: In multitudine populi dignitas regis,  et in paucitate plebis ignominia principis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּרׇב־עָ֥ם הַדְרַת־מֶ֑לֶךְ וּבְאֶ֥פֶס לְ֝אֹ֗ם מְחִתַּ֥ת רָזֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: In the multitude of people is the dignity of the king: and in the small number of the people the dishonour of the prince.
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska kuninkaalla on paljo väkeä, se on hänen kunniansa; vaan koska vähä on väkeä, se tekee päämiehen kehnoksi.
verses.king_james: In the multitude of people is the king’s honour: but in the want of people is the destruction of the prince.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui patiens est multa gubernatur prudentia ;  qui autem impatiens est exaltat stultitiam suam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶ֣רֶךְ אַ֭פַּיִם רַב־תְּבוּנָ֑ה וּקְצַר־ר֝֗וּחַ מֵרִ֥ים אִוֶּֽלֶת׃
verses.douay_rheims: He that is patient, is governed with much wisdom: but he that is impatient, exalteth his folly.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka on pitkämielinen, se on viisas; vaan joka äkillinen on, se ilmoittaa tyhmyyden.
verses.king_james: He that is slow to wrath is of great understanding: but he that is hasty of spirit exalteth folly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vita carnium sanitas cordis ;  putredo ossium invidia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חַיֵּ֣י בְ֭שָׂרִים לֵ֣ב מַרְפֵּ֑א וּרְקַ֖ב עֲצָמ֣וֹת קִנְאָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Soundness of heart is the life of the flesh: but envy is the rottenness of the bones.
verses.finnish_biblia: Leppyinen sydän on ruumiin elämä; vaan kateus on märkä luissa.
verses.king_james: A sound heart is the life of the flesh: but envy the rottenness of the bones.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui calumniatur egentem exprobrat factori ejus ;  honorat autem eum qui miseretur pauperis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֹ֣שֵֽׁק דָּ֭ל חֵרֵ֣ף עֹשֵׂ֑הוּ וּ֝מְכַבְּד֗וֹ חֹנֵ֥ן אֶבְיֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: He that oppresseth the poor, upbraideth his maker: but he that hath pity on the poor, honoureth him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka köyhälle tekee väkivaltaa, hän laittaa hänen luojaansa; vaan joka armahtaa vaivaista, se kunnioittaa Jumalaa.
verses.king_james: He that oppresseth the poor reproacheth his Maker: but he that honoureth him hath mercy on the poor.

verses.latin_vulgate: In malitia sua expelletur impius :  sperat autem justus in morte sua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּֽ֭רָעָתוֹ יִדָּחֶ֣ה רָשָׁ֑ע וְחֹסֶ֖ה בְמוֹת֣וֹ צַדִּֽיק׃
verses.douay_rheims: The wicked man shall be driven out in his wickedness: but the just hath hope in his death.
verses.finnish_biblia: Pahuutensa tähden jumalatoin kukistetaan; vaan vanhurskas on kuolemassakin rohkia.
verses.king_james: The wicked is driven away in his wickedness: but the righteous hath hope in his death.

verses.latin_vulgate: In corde prudentis requiescit sapientia,  et indoctos quosque erudiet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּלֵ֣ב נָ֭בוֹן תָּנ֣וּחַ חׇכְמָ֑ה וּבְקֶ֥רֶב כְּ֝סִילִ֗ים תִּוָּדֵֽעַ׃
verses.douay_rheims: In the heart of the prudent resteth wisdom, and it shall instruct all the ignorant.
verses.finnish_biblia: Toimellisen sydämessä lepää viisaus; mutta mitä tyhmäin mielessä on, se tulee ilmi.
verses.king_james: Wisdom resteth in the heart of him that hath understanding: but that which is in the midst of fools is made known.

verses.latin_vulgate: Justitia elevat gentem ;  miseros autem facit populos peccatum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: צְדָקָ֥ה תְרוֹמֵֽם־גּ֑וֹי וְחֶ֖סֶד לְאֻמִּ֣ים חַטָּֽאת׃
verses.douay_rheims: Justice exalteth a nation: but sin maketh nations miserable.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vanhurskaus korottaa kansan, vaan synti on kansan häpiä.
verses.king_james: Righteousness exalteth a nation: but sin is a reproach to any people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Acceptus est regi minister intelligens ;  iracundiam ejus inutilis sustinebit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רְֽצוֹן־מֶ֭לֶךְ לְעֶ֣בֶד מַשְׂכִּ֑יל וְ֝עֶבְרָת֗וֹ תִּהְיֶ֥ה מֵבִֽישׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: A wise servant is acceptable to the king: he that is good for nothing shall feel his anger.
verses.finnish_biblia: Toimellinen palvelia on kuninkaalle otollinen; vaan häpiällistä palveliaa ei hän kärsi.
verses.king_james: The king’s favour is toward a wise servant: but his wrath is against him that causeth shame.
